narrative: You spot a flat rosette of broad oval leaves growing at the edge of the woodland path...
interesting_fact: Native Americans called it White Man's Footprint because it spread wherever European settlers went — following their paths and roadsides across the world.

questions.question: Which of these plants is Broadleaf Plantain?
Does Broadleaf Plantain have any other common names?
What is the botanical name of Broadleaf Plantain?
Where is Broadleaf Plantain typically found?
options: Almost anywhere — paths, lawns, disturbed and compacted ground
questions.correct: Almost anywhere — paths, lawns, disturbed and compacted ground

questions.question: Select all key identifying features of Broadleaf Plantain
options: Large oval leaves in a flat rosette with prominent parallel veins
Broken stem reveals string-like fibres similar to celery
Heart shaped leaves in groups of three
Tiny hooked hairs that cling to clothing
correct: Large oval leaves in a flat rosette with prominent parallel veins
Broken stem reveals string-like fibres similar to celery

questions.question: What is Broadleaf Plantain used for? Select all that apply
options: Young leaves eaten raw or cooked
Leaves used as a trail nibble — sweet and juicy
correct: Young leaves eaten raw or cooked

questions.question: What does Broadleaf Plantain taste like?
options: Bitter and slightly vegetal — young leaves are best
questions.correct: Bitter and slightly vegetal — young leaves are best

questions.question: What are the cautions with Broadleaf Plantain?
options: No known adverse reactions — avoid older leaves as they become very fibrous
questions.correct: No known adverse reactions — avoid older leaves as they become very fibrous

questions.question: Which species could Broadleaf Plantain be confused with?
options: Ribwort Plantain (Plantago lanceolata) — similar but narrow lance-shaped leaves
questions.correct: Ribwort Plantain (Plantago lanceolata) — similar but narrow lance-shaped leaves

questions.question: Is Broadleaf Plantain native to the UK?
options: It was introduced by Roman settlers

questions.question: What was Broadleaf Plantain historically used to treat? Select all that apply
How was Broadleaf Plantain applied medicinally? Select all that apply
options: Fresh leaves crushed and applied directly as a poultice
Made into a tea or infusion
Ground into a powder and swallowed dry
correct: Fresh leaves crushed and applied directly as a poultice
Made into a tea or infusion

questions.question: What is the life cycle of Broadleaf Plantain?
options: Biennial — takes two years to complete
Ephemeral — only lasts a few weeks

questions.question: What plant family does Broadleaf Plantain belong to?
What is the origin of the name Plantago?
options: From Latin planta meaning sole of the foot — referring to its flat rosette
Named after the botanist Pierre Plantago
questions.correct: From Latin planta meaning sole of the foot — referring to its flat rosette

questions.question: What does Major mean in the botanical name Plantago major?
options: Greater or larger — distinguishing it from smaller plantain species
Medicinal — referring to its herbal uses
Common — referring to how widespread it is
Ancient — referring to how long it has been used
questions.correct: Greater or larger — distinguishing it from smaller plantain species
